Code Assignment.rule: Assign code(s) from Q00-Q99 when a congenital malformation, deformation, or chromosomal abnormality is documented.
Code Assignment.principal_or_secondary: May be assigned as principal/first-listed diagnosis or secondary diagnosis.

When No Unique Code Exists.rule: Assign additional code(s) for any present manifestations if there is no unique code for the anomaly.

Coding Manifestations.rule: Do not code separately manifestations that are inherent components of the anomaly when the code identifies the anomaly.
Coding Manifestations.exception: Assign additional codes for manifestations not inherent to the anomaly.

Congenital malformations, deformations,.Use Throughout Life: Chapter 17 codes may be used throughout the patient’s life.

Corrected Anomalies.rule: If malformation or deformity has been corrected, use a personal history code to identify history.

Late Identification.rule: If anomaly is identified later in life, assign Q00-Q99 code when diagnosed by provider.

Birth Admission Sequencing.rule: For birth admission, assign appropriate Z38 code first, followed by Q00-Q99 codes for congenital anomalies.
